title: Donchian Channel Turtle Breakout
idea: A break above an N-bar high means buyers have absorbed every willing seller, leaving a supply vacuum that sustains the move (the Turtle experiment, Dennis & Eckhardt 1983).
signal_rule: Enter long when close breaks the prior 20-bar high; exit on the prior 10-bar low.

trend_gbm.regime: Geometric Brownian Motion, +15%/yr drift — a persistent uptrend.
trend_gbm.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.05 (CAGR -0.1%) — a marginal regime for this edge; the result is too weak to confirm or reject the thesis. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

mean_rev_ou.regime: Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process oscillating around 100 — pure mean reversion.
mean_rev_ou.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.08 (CAGR +0.2%) — a marginal regime for this edge; the result is too weak to confirm or reject the thesis. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

regime_switch.regime: 200-bar blocks alternating trend and mean reversion — punishes regime overfitting.
regime_switch.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.38 (CAGR +4.1%) — a favourable regime for this edge; this matches what the thesis predicts. Out-of-sample, 60% of walk-forward folds were profitable.

fat_tail.regime: Zero-drift Student-t(3) returns — extreme daily moves ~2–4× more often than normal.
fat_tail.interpretation: In-sample Sharpe +0.16 (CAGR +1.2%) — a favourable regime for this edge; the thesis makes no strong prediction here. Out-of-sample, 40% of walk-forward folds were profitable.
